text.title: lb-high-availability Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for lb-high-availability as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Single-AZ backend deployment behind a multi-AZ load balancer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If all backends are in one AZ and that AZ fails, the LB is healthy but all backends are unreachable — the service is down despite appearing to have HA infrastructure. Spread backends across at least 2 AZs.

failure-mode.description: Setting Route 53 TTL to 30 seconds and claiming sub-minute GSLB failover.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DNS caching by intermediary resolvers and client OS is unpredictable. Corporate DNS, carrier DNS, and CDN edge nodes ignore low TTLs or have minimum cache times. Real-world failover for DNS-based GSLB is 30 seconds to 5 minutes — design SLOs accordingly or switch to anycast.
